blurb: Find My uses an end-to-end encrypted crowd-sourced network to locate lost devices without revealing location data to Apple or reporting devices. Each lost device broadcasts Bluetooth Low Energy advertisements containing a rotating ephemeral public key derived from a device-specific EC P-224 key pair. Nearby devices encrypt the found device's GPS location using ECIES with the ephemeral public key and upload the encrypted report to Apple. Only the owner's device – holding the matching private key – can decrypt these location reports. Keys rotate every 15 minutes to prevent third-party tracking, ensuring privacy for both the lost device owner and the reporting bystander.
